park city.notes: Park City Mountain is on Epic; Deer Valley is largely private/on-Ikon partners depending on pass product—verify current pass maps before buying.

deer valley.notes: Deer Valley skews Ikon/Alterra ecosystem for many skiers; always confirm the exact pass for the season you travel.

breckenridge.notes: Breckenridge and Keystone are Epic; Copper is Ikon—short drive.

keystone.notes: Keystone/Breck on Epic; A-Basin is often Epic or independent—check current year.

aspen.notes: Aspen Snowmass is Ikon destination; not Epic.

tahoe.notes: Palisades and Sierra-at-Tahoe are Ikon; Northstar/Heavenly/Kirkwood are Epic.

lake tahoe.notes: Palisades and Sierra-at-Tahoe are Ikon; Northstar California and Heavenly are Epic.

jackson.notes: Jackson Hole is on Ikon (with restrictions); not Epic.

telluride.notes: Telluride partners with Ikon; verify blackout/restricted days.

big sky.notes: Big Sky is Ikon; not Epic.

vermont.notes: VT has both ecosystems; match the specific resort.

utah.notes: Utah is mixed: Alta/Snowbird often Ikon; Park City area Epic+Ikon—choose mountain first.

hawaii.notes: No downhill skiing; winter here is surf/hike weather.

vegas.notes: Las Vegas is not a major Epic/Ikon hub; consider Utah or CA road trips for real ski weeks.

las vegas.notes: Small local hill or longer drives to UT/CO for pass value.
